name: SOL Session Drive Continuation v1
thesis: SOL 1h is the best-fit adjacent lane for testing opening-range or session-drive continuation because the mechanism is inherently intraday and should generate better density than forcing it onto the slower 4h primary lane.
invalidation_condition: Abort if impulse-hold behavior stays too rare, if momentum and hold filters conflict, or if continuation does not survive costs in blind windows.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: An intraday impulse hour establishes structure and follow-through persists when the next bar holds rather than fully mean reverting.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition regimes can produce temporary inventory imbalance after an impulsive session move, especially when the first retrace fails to fully erase the drive.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 1h is the right lane because session-window continuation is a faster mechanism and SOL usually provides enough intraday amplitude for dense participation.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the impulse bars usually fully mean revert within one bar, the hold logic will enter too early into failed continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries appear but follow-through is short, shorten the hold horizon and de-risk faster before tightening the trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; momentum improvement and structural hold are aligned
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: reduced by using 1h instead of 4h for a session-driven thesis

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
